data.title: Over 50% of Americans Made 'Risky Financial Stopgaps' Last Year — 20% Even Skipped Medical Care
data.description:
Quick Summary

  • More than half of Americans used at least one risky financial stopgap late last year, with many cutting essentials, missing debt payments or even skipping medical care to stay afloat.
  • For borrowers buried under high-interest balances, you can compare lenders and personalized loan options in minutes without affecting credit scores.

For a growing share of Americans, the numbers don't line up anymore.

A survey issued by Achieve found that in the last three months of 2025, 51% resorted to at least one "risky financial stopgap" after falling short on what they already owed. Half of those who took action cut spending on basic needs, 34% leaned harder on credit cards and 26% pulled money from emergency or short‑term savings.

"This is what the K-shaped economy looks like in the real world," said Achieve Co-CEO Andrew Housser. "There's an affluent half of the population whose financial lives aren't disrupted by momentary inconveniences. But for everyone else, financial triage and tradeoffs are a way of life."

Belt‑tightening down to the last notch

The survey suggests many households have already exhausted easy ways to cut back. More than 9 in 10 said they could only reduce housing costs, bills and utilities, groceries, or transportation "slightly" or not at all. Even in discretionary areas, most people feel tapped out.

Seventy five percent said they were out of room to cut entertainment at home, 59% said the same about gift‑giving, 56% about entertainment outside the home and 55% about vacations. When the budget side is fully squeezed, the only remaining lever is often the cost of existing debt, not whether you owe it, but what rate you're paying and how it's structured.

"Many consumers' belt‑tightening efforts have reached the last notch," Housser noted, warning that staying current on debts gets harder when there's no slack left and prices or payments rise. That lack of margin also makes households more vulnerable to routine surprises like car repairs or medical bills.​

The survey backs that up. Fifty‑five percent of respondents said they carry credit card balances to cover essential expenses, and more than a quarter have kept those balances for at least six months. 

Overall, 55% rated their financial situation as "Poor" or "Fair," while just 13% said "Excellent."​

Debt trends show slow progress 

Achieve's report does show some mixed signals. Over the prior three months, 24% of respondents saw their total debt increase, while 32% saw it decrease and 44% said it stayed the same. That's a slight improvement from the prior quarter, when 27% reported rising debt and 42% said it was flat.

Put together, the data sets an illustration that some households are slowly digging out, but a large share are stuck cycling balances just to keep up with essentials, with little room for error.

A more deliberate way to reset

There's no single fix for a cost‑of‑living squeeze. But when high‑interest debt is at the center of the problem, the order of operations matters:

Get a clear picture of what you owe and to whom. Prioritize the most expensive balances and the most urgent obligations, and look for ways to lower the cost of that debt—through rate reductions, structured payoff plans—before it forces more skipped bills or medical delays.

This is where some households turn to personal loans or consolidation loans to replace multiple high‑interest balances with a single, fixed payment. 

In a year when more than half of Americans say they've already resorted to risky stopgaps, moving from reactive choices to deliberate ones may be an important shift.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Kratos, Airbus Collaborate On Advanced Uncrewed Combat Aircraft
data.description:
Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:KTOS) shares are up during Friday's premarket session. The company disclosed that it is preparing two Valkyries for their first flight with a European mission system.

This positive movement comes as Kratos continues its collaboration with Airbus SE (OTC:EADSY). The deal entails developing an integrated Uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft (UCCA) for the German Air Force.

Details

The joint effort aims to deliver a proven combat aircraft that integrates with Airbus's Multiplatform Autonomous Reconfigurable and Secure (MARS) system.

The Valkyrie has a range exceeding 5,000 kilometers and is capable of flying at altitudes up to 45,000 feet. It is designed to undertake sensitive missions that would be too dangerous for pilots.

In addition to the technical specifications, the partnership emphasizes affordability and rapid deployment, addressing urgent defense needs in Europe.

The Valkyrie first flew in the U.S. in 2019, with additional aircraft operating regularly since then. The Airbus variant is set to make its maiden flight in 2026.

As the maiden flight approaches, Kratos is preparing two Valkyries in Manching, Germany.

Management Commentary

Steve Fendley, President of Kratos Unmanned Systems Division, added, "By taking the flight-proven and in-production Valkyrie and integrating the Airbus MARS mission system, the Airbus-missionized Valkyrie UCCA is a multi-mission, affordable system that can operate independently, in teams of UAS, or in Manned-Unmanned-Teaming operations."

"Along with the technical and production backing Airbus and Kratos bring, we are realizing an optimal capability system that can be bought and deployed as ‘affordable mass'; the consistent discriminator identified in today's peer to peer wargames."

Technical Analysis

Currently, the stock is trading 1.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 1% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness but longer-term strength. Over the past 12 months, shares have surged by 203.05%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting strong overall performance.

The RSI is at 47.93, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at -2.3361, with the signal line at -2.5083, indicating bullish momentum as the MACD is above the signal line.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is not currently overbought, there is potential for upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $97.00
  • Key Support: $84.50

Analyst Outlook

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $91.47. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Canaccord Genuity: Buy (Raises Target to $125.00) (Feb. 24)
  • BTIG: Buy (Raises Target to $115.00) (Feb. 24)
  • UBS: Initiated with Neutral (Target $79.00) (Feb. 18)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 2.56 — The stock is currently trading at a premium compared to its peers.
  • Growth: 71.17 — Indicates strong growth potential relative to the market.
  • Quality: 86.09 — Reflects a solid balance sheet and operational efficiency.
  • Momentum: 96.06 — Suggests the stock is outperforming the broader market significantly.

The Verdict: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions' Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the market. However, the low value score suggests that investors should be cautious about the premium valuation as they consider potential entry points.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because KTOS carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

KTOS Stock Price Activity: Kratos Defense & Security shares were up 0.49% at $89.90 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Tilly's Turns A Profit Again: Retailer Delivers First Strong Q4 Since 2021
data.description:
Tilly’s Inc. (NASDAQ:TLYS) shares are up during Friday’s premarket session following a strong earnings report that highlighted the company’s first profitable fourth quarter since fiscal 2021.

Sales jumped 5.3% to $155.13 million, beating the consensus of $148.7 million.

The company also noted a 10.1% increase in total comparable net sales, with physical store comps rising 10.3% and e-commerce comps up 9.8%, showcasing strong performance across its retail channels.

Since turning positive in August, Tilly’s has now produced seven consecutive months of comparable net sales growth, including February 2026, which increased by 20 percent, according to CEO Nate Smith.

“We are off to a strong start to fiscal 2026, and we feel optimistic about our prospects for the year,” he added.

Mixed 2026 Forecast

TLYS RSI 87.93 Flags Overbought

The stock is currently trading 65.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 55.4% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating significant short-term strength.

Shares have decreased 9.09% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting a longer-term downtrend despite recent gains.

The RSI is at 87.93, indicating that the stock is in overbought territory, which could suggest a potential pullback. Meanwhile, MACD is at 0.0814, above its signal line at -0.0022, indicating bullish momentum in the short term.

The combination of overbought RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock has seen strong recent performance, traders should be cautious of potential corrections.

  • Key Resistance: $3.00
  • Key Support: $1.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a N/A Rating. No recent analyst actions available.

TLYS Stock Price Activity: Tilly’s shares were trading 2.92% at $2.47 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: SentinelOne Stock Falls After Mixed Q4 Results, Weak Q1 EPS Guidance
data.description:
SentinelOne, Inc. (NYSE:S) shares are trading lower Friday after the company reported mixed fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday after the market closed and issued first-quarter adjusted earnings per share guidance below estimates.

  • SentinelOne stock is feeling bearish pressure. Why is S stock dropping?

Q4 Results And Business Metrics

SentinelOne reported adjusted earnings per share of 7 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 6 cents. In addition, it reported revenue of $271.153 million, missing the consensus estimate of $271.155 million, but representing a 20% year-over-year increase.

Annualized recurring revenue increased 22% to $1.119 billion as of Jan. 31.

Customers with annualized recurring revenue of $100,000 or more grew 18% year-over-year to 1,667 as of Jan. 31.

Cash, cash equivalents and investments totaled $769.6 million as of Jan. 31.

"We surpassed the $1 billion revenue milestone, growing 22% year-over-year, and achieved full-year operating profitability – a strong close to fiscal year '26," said CEO Tomer Weingarten. "Businesses of all sizes, including the world’s largest enterprises, are standardizing on the Singularity platform as the foundation for securing AI and autonomous cybersecurity."

Looking ahead, SentinelOne sees first-quarter adjusted earnings per share of 1 cent to 2 cents, versus the consensus estimate of 5 cents. Furthermore, it sees revenue of $276.00 million to $278.00 million, versus the consensus estimate of $277.03 million.

For the fiscal-year, the company expects adjusted earnings per share of 32 cents to 38 cents, versus the consensus estimate of 30 cents. It also sees revenue of $1.19 billion to $1.205 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $1.204 billion.

SentinelOne Shares Edge Lower

S Price Action: At the time of publication, SentinelOne shares are trading 3.63% lower at $13.28, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: InMode Board Approves Buyback Covering Nearly 10% Of Stock
data.description:
InMode Ltd. (NASDAQ:INMD) shares are up during Friday’s premarket session after the company announced a new share repurchase program.

InMode Authorizes 10% Share Buyback

InMode’s Board of Directors has authorized a new share repurchase program for up to approximately 10% of the company’s total shares outstanding, representing around 6.38 million ordinary shares.

This decision follows the company’s repurchase of approximately $127.4 million of its outstanding shares in 2025, indicating a commitment to returning value to shareholders despite a challenging macroeconomic environment.

The company continues to generate strong cash flow and believes that repurchasing shares at its current valuation is an attractive use of capital.

Management remains focused on navigating ongoing regional conflicts while evaluating additional share repurchase programs and other capital allocation opportunities.

INMD 7.4% Below 50-Day SMA

The stock is currently trading 7.4% below its 50-day simple moving average (SMA) and 8% below its 200-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend. Shares have decreased 30.88% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 29.69, which is considered oversold territory, suggesting a potential rebound. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.4430, below its signal line at -0.3432, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of an oversold RSI and a bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum: while the stock is oversold, the MACD points to continued selling pressure.

  • Key Resistance: $15.00

INMD EPS Seen at $0.37

InMode is slated to provide its next financial update on April 27, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: 37 cents (Up from 31 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $79.32 million (Up from $77.87 million YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 8.9x (Indicates value opportunity)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $25 .00. Recent analyst moves include:

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for InMode, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:
